preview_text:
The model favours Belgium at 54%, with Egypt's win probability at 17%. The underdog faces a roughly three-to-one gap in outright victory odds, positioning Belgium as a clear but not dominant favourite on a neutral ground.

The draw carries material probability at 29% under the model, suggesting a competitive match where neither side is expected to control proceedings decisively. The 27-percentage-point margin between Belgian win and Egyptian win reflects asymmetry in the matchup, yet the combined non-Belgium outcomes (46%) indicate this is not a heavily tilted contest. A stalemate remains a plausible outcome.

The sportsbook consensus shifts Belgium up to 59%, a gap of roughly 5 percentage points above the model. Polymarket prices align closely with the books. The model and market agree on Egypt's chances (17% vs 17%) and draw probability (29% vs 24%). The divergence centres on Belgium's win probability, with the market materially more confident in a Belgian victory. The dashboard offers both estimates without adjudicating between them.
